ud8.1:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud8.1:1.3:
And on that occasion the Blessed One was instructing, urging, rousing,
& encouraging the monks with Dhamma-talk concerned with unbinding.
ud8.1:1.4: The monks–receptive, attentive, focusing their entire awareness, lending ear–listened to the Dhamma.
ud8.1:2.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud8.1:3.2:
And there, I say, there is neither coming, nor going, nor staying;
neither passing away nor arising:
ud8.1:3.4: This, just this, is the end of stress.
